protocol.pseudo_generation: multiple strategies in response-matrix space, generated only from held-out known-normal matrices
protocol.diagnostic_labels: true anomaly labels are used only to form real-anomaly/test-normal diagnostic groups

gate_definitions.G1_alignment: On key readouts, pseudo-real Wasserstein / normal-real Wasserstein <= 1.10 for at least half of available key features.
gate_definitions.G2_difficulty: Normal-pseudo distance must be in [0.55, 1.80] × normal-real distance, avoiding too-easy OOD pseudo anomalies and too-weak near-normal pseudo anomalies.
gate_definitions.G3_direction: Pseudo-minus-normal mean shift must have the same sign as real-minus-normal shift for at least half of key features.
gate_definitions.G4_readout_relevance: Best diagnostic AUC among readouts remains within 0.03 of mat_mean or exceeds mat_mean, meaning the fixed matrix regime still contains the known anomaly signal.

summary.interpretation_hint: If normal_pseudo distance is much larger than normal_real or pseudo-real distance remains large on high-response features, pseudo anomalies are over-strong / misaligned relative to real anomalies.
